title: Tamil Chicken Salna

subs.name: 500 g mixed vegetables (potato, carrot, peas) for a vegetarian salna
60 ml coconut milk added with the water at step 5

ingredients.name: cashews (for paste)
visual_checks: adds body and richness to the paste
ingredients.notes: adds body and richness to the paste

subs.name: omit entirely or use 2 tbsp coconut milk for body
a small handful of coriander leaves added at the same stage

steps.text: Grind the coconut with cashews (if using) and fennel seeds with 4 tablespoons of water to a very smooth paste. Set aside. This paste is the body of the salna.
Heat oil in a kadai over medium heat. Add cinnamon, cloves and cardamom. When fragrant, add curry leaves and the sliced onions. Fry, stirring, until the onions are golden and soft, about 7 minutes.
Add ginger-garlic paste and fry for 2 minutes. Add the tomatoes and cook until they break down completely and oil starts to separate, about 5 minutes.
Lower the flame. Add red chilli powder, coriander powder and turmeric. Stir for 30 seconds, then add the chicken pieces. Toss well to coat in the masala and sear on medium-high for 4 minutes.
Pour in 500 ml hot water and salt. The gravy should be quite thin at this stage. Bring to a boil, then cover and simmer on medium for 15 minutes until the chicken is cooked through.
Stir in the coconut-cashew-fennel paste and mint leaves if using. Simmer uncovered for 5 minutes until the gravy is lightly thickened but still pourable — salna should flow, not stand.
Taste and correct the salt. Scatter coriander leaves and serve immediately alongside parotta, biryani or idiyappam — ladling the thin gravy liberally over or alongside the main dish.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Verify poultry doneness with a food thermometer where possible; do not rely on colour alone.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: cashew → omit entirely or use 2 tbsp coconut milk for body; coconut → 60 ml coconut milk added with the water at step 5; chicken → 500 g mixed vegetables (potato, carrot, peas) for a vegetarian salna; mint → a small handful of coriander leaves added at the same stage
